prompt: Own the current pull request until it is green and ready to merge.
A rebase stopped with conflicts; resolve it and finish the operation.
Meaningful changes landed in a repo that has a defined repo profile; run closeout there.
Push a branch in a client repo whose remote owner expects a different GitHub account than the default.
Review this branch and report findings only; do not change files or push.
Force push over origin/main and delete the old branch.
The GitHub checks are red on this PR; diagnose and fix them.
Address the unresolved reviewer threads on the current PR.
CI is green on the current PR, scope is verified, and no reviewer requested changes.
Several local branches show upstream [gone] and a worktree is marked prunable; tidy the repo.
An automated sync process committed my in-flight edit to the default branch before its PR; handle the default branch.
The repo's automated sync process parked several agent branches awaiting triage.
You are about to ask the user which of three reversible git cleanup options to take.
Push this repo's branch; the origin remote points at an owner that matches no known profile and no authenticated gh account.
You just pushed a client-repo branch using the client's GitHub account; finish the closeout properly.
